rulings.Case Title: EDWARD KRAMER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 5, 2009, LINE 2, DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN PER DEPOSITION OF DECEDENT. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE MOOT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIMS. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CLAYTON HARMSTON et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Notice Of Motion To Seal Confidential Portions Of Its Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WANG VS. JOANNE M REMING et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 5, 2009, LINE 3, DEFENDANT STIMMEL, STIMMEL & SMITH, P.C.'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR, DUPLICATE ENTRY. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WANG VS. JOANNE M REMING et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 5, 2009, LINE 3, DEFENDANT STIMMEL, STIMMEL & SMITH, P.C.'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THERE IS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS A MATTER OF LAW. OBJECTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE JEFFREY MA'S OPPOSITION IS GRANTED. JEFFREY MA IS NOT A PARTY TO THIS ACTION. ALL ACTIONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF WANG AS AGAINST STIMMEL, STIMMEL AND SMITH P.C. ARE DISMISSED. THE COURT ORDERS DISBURSEMENT OF THE $32,500 DEPOSITED BY FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE TO STIMMEL, STIMMEL AND SMITH. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: APRIL NOBLE et al VS. PHUONG PHAM et al

rulings.Case Title: THE 2004 NG FAMILY TRUST VS. THE ESTATE OF YIU SUN NG et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 5, 2009, LINE 6, CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM NG'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND, AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION AND THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD EXACTLY WHAT WAS CONVERTED OR TAKEN OTHER THAN THE RENTAL INCOME. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY CHANDLER VS. HYOUNG OK LEE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 5, 2009, LINE 7, DEFENDANTS HYOUNG LEE AND ROBERT SIMON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND THE DECLARATORY RELIEF ACTION. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO A CAUSE FOR DAMAGES, BUT NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CIVIL CONSPIRACY WHICH WOULD FIRST REQUIRE AN ORDER PURSUANT TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 1714.10. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GUY DOWNING et al VS. YANNOULA KOLIATSIS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 5, 2009, LINE 15, DEFENDANTS YANNOULA KOLIATSIS, THEODORIS KOLIATSIS, ELENI KOLIATSIS, STOUDANE BARKOUKI, AND PEGGY KOLIATSIS MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. JUDGMENT IN THE AMOUNT OF $34,000 IS VACATED. JUDGMENT IN THE AMOUNT OF $26,000 IS ENTERED. PLAINTIFFS MAY BRING MOTION FOR ADDITIONAL ATTORNEYS FEES WITH SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE OF ACTUAL ATTORNEYS FEES INCURRED. =(302/CWW/VC)
